provenance: AUTHORED (no real memoir exists yet — the quarterly lambda has not produced its first record). Facts dict is real-shaped per coach_memoir_lambda._render_facts_for_prompt; the text cites the refuted deep_sleep call by name.

misses.claim: deep sleep would rebound above 1.4h within a week

hits.claim: recovery would hold above 60% through the deficit

stance_start.headline_read: sleep architecture is the limiting factor

stance_end.headline_read: consistency, not architecture, is the limiting factor
stance_end.how_my_read_changed: the data refuted my deep-sleep rebound call

reference_output: Fourteen graded calls this quarter, and the ledger reads 5 confirmed against 7 refuted — a 41.7% hit rate across the 12 that decided. The one I keep returning to is deep_sleep: I said it would rebound, and I was wrong. I opened the quarter convinced sleep architecture was the limiting factor; I close it believing consistency is. That correction cost me 7 refuted calls to learn.

provenance: AUTHORED — the no-refuted-learnings path: nothing to cite, so the miss gate has nothing to demand.
reference_output: Three calls, three confirmed — a 100% hit rate on a small book of 3. I will not pretend a perfect quarter at this sample size is mastery; it is mostly a quarter of conservative calls that the data happened to reward.
